verses.text: The people weep and would choose a captain to return to Egypt. Rebellion. They despise the promised land.
The people weep and would choose a captain to return to Egypt. Rebellion. They despise the promised land.
The people weep and would choose a captain to return to Egypt. Rebellion. They despise the promised land.
The people weep and would choose a captain to return to Egypt. Rebellion. They despise the promised land.
Joshua and Caleb plead, 'the LORD is with us; fear them not.' Faith. Two stand against the crowd.
Joshua and Caleb plead, 'the LORD is with us; fear them not.' Faith. Two stand against the crowd.
Joshua and Caleb plead, 'the LORD is with us; fear them not.' Faith. Two stand against the crowd.
Joshua and Caleb plead, 'the LORD is with us; fear them not.' Faith. Two stand against the crowd.
Joshua and Caleb plead, 'the LORD is with us; fear them not.' Faith. Two stand against the crowd.
The congregation would stone them; the glory appears. Threatened. Faith is met with fury.
God threatens to disinherit them and make Moses a nation. Wrath. Unbelief provokes God.
God threatens to disinherit them and make Moses a nation. Wrath. Unbelief provokes God.
Moses pleads that the nations not say God could not bring them in. His name. He pleads for God's honour.
Moses pleads that the nations not say God could not bring them in. His name. He pleads for God's honour.
Moses pleads that the nations not say God could not bring them in. His name. He pleads for God's honour.
Moses pleads that the nations not say God could not bring them in. His name. He pleads for God's honour.
He asks pardon 'according unto thy great mercy.' Mercy. He appeals to God's character.
He asks pardon 'according unto thy great mercy.' Mercy. He appeals to God's character.
He asks pardon 'according unto thy great mercy.' Mercy. He appeals to God's character.
God pardons, but 'all the earth shall be filled with my glory.' Pardoned. Mercy spares, yet justice stands.
God pardons, but 'all the earth shall be filled with my glory.' Pardoned. Mercy spares, yet justice stands.
None who tempted Him ten times shall see the land. Barred. The faithless lose the promise.
None who tempted Him ten times shall see the land. Barred. The faithless lose the promise.
None who tempted Him ten times shall see the land. Barred. The faithless lose the promise.
None who tempted Him ten times shall see the land. Barred. The faithless lose the promise.
All numbered over twenty shall die in the wilderness. Forty years. A generation falls for unbelief.
All numbered over twenty shall die in the wilderness. Forty years. A generation falls for unbelief.
All numbered over twenty shall die in the wilderness. Forty years. A generation falls for unbelief.
All numbered over twenty shall die in the wilderness. Forty years. A generation falls for unbelief.
All numbered over twenty shall die in the wilderness. Forty years. A generation falls for unbelief.
Their children shall enter, but they wander forty years. Wandering. The cost of unbelief is long.
Their children shall enter, but they wander forty years. Wandering. The cost of unbelief is long.
Their children shall enter, but they wander forty years. Wandering. The cost of unbelief is long.
Their children shall enter, but they wander forty years. Wandering. The cost of unbelief is long.
Their children shall enter, but they wander forty years. Wandering. The cost of unbelief is long.
The evil spies die by plague; Joshua and Caleb live. Judged. The fearmongers fall.
The evil spies die by plague; Joshua and Caleb live. Judged. The fearmongers fall.
The evil spies die by plague; Joshua and Caleb live. Judged. The fearmongers fall.
The people mourn and presume to go up the mountain. Presumption. Too late they would obey.
The people mourn and presume to go up the mountain. Presumption. Too late they would obey.
They go without God and are smitten by Amalek. Defeat. Without God they fail.
They go without God and are smitten by Amalek. Defeat. Without God they fail.
